metadata.name: Museum Pieces #4
metadata.description:
A premium collection of handmade art featuring Women, Janitors, Wiener Dogs, Orcas, Giraffes, and a suckling Pig. 

No shirts, no shoes, no p5.js, no problem. 

Please keep your voices down and stay off your phones while in the museum as others are here to take in the art. 



Special thanks to @purespider, @Iteration__, and @jamin81NFT for their help.
tags: Zumba Classes for your out of shape stepmom

metadata.name: Uninhabitable #111
metadata.description:
Uninhabitable is a generative system built only from ellipses. The process of playing with different geometric and trigonometric disruptions gives rise to four different compositions which seek to evoque living constructions and environments. Besides the shape itself and its related distortion, the stroke weight of the lines plays a meaningful role in the piece. Following a discrete range [0.01,0.1,1] the weight reveals the process allowing the viewer to realize the trace of the huge amount of lines in each piece. This back and forth between scales and context is, while kind of stripping the code, part of my continuing interest on understanding the -usually- crazy paths of machines' way of thinking.

Press "h" to see it and high resolution and refresh to get back to the initial state. Press "h" to save it.

Uninhabitable is an original work by Iskra Velitchkova. 2021.

metadata.name: Miniscapes #68
metadata.description:
Miniscapes merges generative code (Rich's bit) with meticulously designed scenes and elements (Rick's bit), to create minimalist landscapes intended to charm, delight, and surprise.

Our aim was to convey as much as possible with as little as possible by using simple shapes and lines to represent a wide variety of natural landscapes.

We hope that you find as much joy in owning a Miniscape as we did in creating them.

Controls (in live view):
- Press [s] to save the current image
- Press [p] to pause an animated output
- Press [r] to reset an animated output (this will also pause)

metadata.name: Garden, Monoliths #246
metadata.description:
"Garden, Monoliths" is the result of an algorithm that started in April 2021. It was called "Grass.js".
Spring was blooming all around the house, taking over the stones and remnants of ancient battles that stood here. Inspiration was everywhere.
The initial goal was to use mathematical formulas for creating patterns inspired by the density of nature, that could be used by a pen plotter, so it kept focusing on outputting vector lines. This history and practice produced a graphical style based on line strokes, plus occasionally some transparent, filled shapes.
Each artwork will be either "Garden" or "Monolith", for they are variations of two existing designs from the workshop.

metadata.name: de|growth:generations #8
metadata.description:
Materials tend to take particular forms due to their physical properties, technological necessities or natural limitations. However, what rules should a simulated environment follow when no physical constraints are inherent to the digital environment?

This project continues the exploration of shaping digital matter that I began on hicetnunc in 2021. Each token creates an image of a unique sculpture generated with a custom growth/degrowth algorithm. The image is rendered with a tailored raytracing script.

---
Depending on the device and CPU usage, it takes a few seconds (on fast computers) to a minute (sometimes more) on mid-tier phones for the 800x800px image.

Click on the image to open the menu. You may choose the resolution from preview quality to a print of approx. 30x30cm. There may be minor differences in tone or contrast for different resolutions.

In live mode:
Click PNG to save the rendered image.
Click STL to download the digital model to view on your computer or 3d print at home [experimental]

metadata.name: fxBLOCK #98
metadata.description:
fxBLOCK is a collection of speculative architectures. 
Each minted BLOCK is constructed using a set of polyvalent parts in various deterministic tectonic configurations, lights, and moods. 

fx(hash) genesis Generative Token by @grasser_alex 2021

metadata.name: SYSTEM_HASH #56
metadata.description:
generative universes containing arbitrary matter with arbitrary properties // emergent physics unique to each piece

note: because this is a physics simulation the screen coordinates are important with regard to the plane the objects exist on and so the script is deterministic _as long as you use the same window dimensions_, this also means you can play around with different sizes.

metadata.name: Euphonic #693
metadata.description:
Euphonic is an exploration of the emergent visual properties that arise from placing layers of simple lines atop one another.

In general this effect is called the Moiré effect. This effect really feels best in the tactile - when you have a printed page and an acetate sheet. This piece attempts to recreate that tactile joy.

This is built in WebGL 2 and so doesn't work in Safari 14 and below. All latest browsers will work as expected.
